rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no 'fixed' retrospectives, no session biography
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:143|Stale markers (2026-YYYY-MM-DD) appear only in maintainer block, exempt

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match — Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa)
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:13|'The feeling, files materialising in two folders at once, four windows churning on one question, is the lesson.' (Sutherland counterintuitive reframe + sensory detail)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique
rules_evaluated.evidence: No unattributed techniques in body prose; framework credits confined to maintainer block (line 149)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:5|'retriever' earned by function; line:9|'synthesizer' earned as 'the fourth (the synthesizer)'; line:9|'memory' earned as 'the curated layer'; line:17|'crux' earned from prior module reference
line:21|'Three retrievers is the default; if you can't get one running today...The lesson works with two retrievers, even one.' — architectural framing (design resilience), not apologetic reassurance

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + strategy docs, NOT in student-facing body
rules_evaluated.evidence: No value-prop leak detected; 'vendor' appears only in fenced prompt (line 89, allowable descriptor)
Exercise names what students do: run four agents in parallel; feel concurrency.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:5|'retriever' + 'Hire three agents to fetch'; line:9|'synthesizer' + 'reads...and integrates'; all terms earned operationally
line:9|'memory/' distinguished as curated persistent layer vs 'sources/' as transient input
Body prose contains no em-dashes; maintainer block exempt

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:45|Session 1 prompt lacks lead-in (label appears at line 45 without preceding semantic sentence); line:66|Session 2 prompt; line:87|Session 3 prompt; line:109|'Push another round' prompt has lead-in at line 107; line:120|Session 4 prompt lacks lead-in
rules_evaluated.fix: Add one-sentence action lead-in before each prompt's **Prompt** label. Example: 'Ask Claude to propose wiki search terms, wait for your edits, then search and extract findings.' (20 words max, command verb, semantic summary). Required for lines 45, 66, 87, 120. Reusable prompt at line 109 already has a lead-in.

rules_evaluated.evidence: line:52|'sources/wiki-retrieval.md'; line:73|'sources/docs-retrieval.md'; line:94|'sources/internet-retrieval.md'; line:131|'memory/_synthesis-m3.md' — all explicit subpaths

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't restate session CWD inside the prompt body
rules_evaluated.evidence: Prompts use relative subpaths ('sources/', 'memory/'); no CWD restatement or absolute paths within fences
line:13|'The feeling, files materialising in two folders at once, four windows churning on one question, is the lesson.' — mood framing explicit and honors module's emotional arc

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode
rules_evaluated.evidence: Title and Big Idea name the shape: 'Three retrievers, one curator' — discipline of parallel retrieval + synthesis

summary.key_finding: Four prompt blocks (Session 1, 2, 3, and Session 4) lack required one-sentence action lead-ins before their **Prompt** labels.

blocking_issues.issue: Missing semantic lead-ins on four primary prompts (lines 45, 66, 87, 120)
blocking_issues.fix: Add one-sentence summary (max 20 words, command verb) immediately before each prompt label. Example pattern: 'Ask Claude to propose X search terms, wait for your edits, then search and extract findings.'
